builder.role: Primary implementer using Claude Code for TDD development

reviewer.review_focus: - Security vulnerabilities - Performance issues - Best practices adherence - Test coverage adequacy - Code maintainability

fixer.fix_capabilities: - Root cause analysis - Targeted remediation - Pattern-based solutions - Performance optimization - Security hardening

critic.role: Final quality validation before story completion
critic.validation_scope: - Story acceptance criteria met - Code quality standards passed - Test coverage adequate - Documentation complete - No outstanding issues

available_tools.description: You have access to the following tools for executing this task. Tool mappings are defined in .claude/tools/tool-mappings.xml and full documentation is in .claude/tools/available-tools.xml

tool.name: Read
tool.description: Read story context and existing code

tool.name: github_mcp
tool.usage: Branch management, code commits, PR creation

tool.name: postgres_mcp
tool.usage: Test database setup, schema changes, RLS policies

tool.name: supabase_mcp
tool.usage: Deploy migrations, update types after schema changes

tool.name: browser_tools_mcp
tool.usage: Test UI components, check accessibility, debug errors

tool.name: newrelic_mcp
tool.usage: Check performance impact, debug production issues

tool.name: context7_mcp
tool.usage: Get current API docs for libraries being used

available_tools.tool_usage_guidelines: - Use Read extensively to understand existing patterns - Write tests FIRST (TDD approach) - Use Bash to run tests frequently during development - Use postgres_mcp for LOCAL database testing - Use browser_tools_mcp for UI component testing - Use github_mcp to create feature branches and commits - Use context7_mcp when implementing new library features - Use TodoWrite to track progress through story tasks

prompt.task: Implement a user story following Test-Driven Development practices and project standards, creating production-ready code with proper testing, error handling, and documentation.

step.title: Analyze Story Requirements
step.actions: - Review acceptance criteria from story context - Identify all technical tasks to implement - Understand user personas and their needs - Map out dependencies between tasks

step.title: Study Existing Patterns
step.actions: - Find similar implementations in codebase - Identify reusable components and utilities - Understand naming conventions and file structure - Note any anti-patterns to avoid

step.title: Plan Implementation Approach
step.actions: - Break down into incremental steps - Identify what tests to write first - Plan component hierarchy (Server vs Client) - Document technical decisions

step.title: Write Failing Tests First
step.actions: - Create test files following naming convention - Write unit tests for business logic - Write integration tests for user workflows - Ensure tests fail initially (Red phase)

step.title: Implement Minimum Code
step.actions: - Write just enough code to pass tests - Follow existing patterns strictly - Use proper TypeScript types (no 'any') - Implement security measures (RLS, validation)

step.title: Refactor for Quality
step.actions: - Extract reusable functions - Improve code organization - Add proper error handling - Optimize performance if needed - Ensure all tests still pass (Green phase)

step.title: Follow Component Patterns
Implement Security Standards
step.actions: - Always use Row Level Security (RLS) - Never expose API keys in client code - Validate all inputs with Zod schemas - Use enhanceAction for server actions - Implement proper authentication checks

step.title: Add Error Handling
step.actions: - Catch and handle expected errors - Provide user-friendly error messages - Log technical details for debugging - Use custom error classes

step.title: Run Quality Checks
step.commands:
# Run all tests pnpm test
# Run specific test file pnpm test apps/web/app/home/(user)/editor/_actions/generate-content.test.ts
# Check TypeScript types pnpm typecheck
# Fix linting issues pnpm lint:fix
# Check for console.log statements grep -r "console.log" apps/web/app/home/(user)/editor/

step.title: Update Documentation
step.actions: - Add JSDoc comments to exported functions - Update component prop types - Document any new patterns introduced - Update API documentation if needed

step.title: Performance Validation
step.checks: - Ensure no N+1 queries - Check bundle size impact - Verify loading states work properly - Test error scenarios - Validate accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)

step.title: Update Progress Documentation
step.template:
# Story Progress: {Story Title}
## Current Status - **Phase**: Implementation - **Completion**: 75% complete - **Last Session**: 2024-01-15 - **Next Session Plan**: Complete integration tests
## Completed Tasks - [x] Create generateContentAction with Zod validation - 2024-01-15 - [x] Implement ContentGeneratorDialog component - 2024-01-15 - [x] Add AI usage tracking - 2024-01-15 - [ ] Write integration tests - [ ] Add loading states and error handling
## Technical Decisions
### 2024-01-15: AI Gateway Choice **Context**: Needed to choose between OpenAI direct or Portkey **Decision**: Use Portkey for unified AI gateway **Reasoning**: Better rate limiting, usage tracking, and provider flexibility
### 2024-01-15: Component Architecture **Context**: Server vs Client component for content generator **Decision**: Server component wrapper with client dialog **Reasoning**: Better performance, secure quota checking
## Files Modified - `apps/web/app/home/(user)/editor/_actions/generate-content.action.ts` - `apps/web/app/home/(user)/editor/_components/ContentGenerator.tsx` - `apps/web/app/home/(user)/editor/_components/ContentGeneratorClient.tsx` - `packages/supabase/src/schema/ai-usage.schema.ts`
## Next Session Preparation - **Focus**: Complete integration tests and error states - **Files to Read**: - `apps/web/app/home/(user)/editor/editor.integration.test.ts` - Similar error handling in `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/`

step.title: Update GitHub Issue
step.actions: - Check off completed tasks in story issue - Add comment with implementation notes - Update Implementation Phase field - Link to any related PRs or issues

best_practices.practice: Always use RLS, never bypass with service role except in admin-only server functions
Every user input must be validated with Zod before processing
Start with Server Components, only use Client Components for interactivity
Write tests before implementation - if tests are hard to write, the design needs work
Users should never see technical error messages - always translate to friendly language
Measure before optimizing - use React Query for data fetching, avoid waterfalls

step.action: Implement story using TDD with Claude Code

criteria.functional_completeness: All acceptance criteria implemented and tested
criteria.code_quality: Passes all linting, type checking, and security scans
criteria.test_coverage: Adequate test coverage for all new code
criteria.documentation: Code is self-documenting with necessary comments
criteria.security: No vulnerabilities, follows security best practices

actions.on_pass: Story marked as complete, ready for deployment
actions.on_fail: Story returned to Builder with specific fixes required

context_package.rollback_plan: How to revert if issues arise
